heading: Leases of unallotted lands for mining purposes; duration of leases
label: 396a Leases of unallotted lands for mining purposes; duration of leases
text: On and after , unallotted lands within any Indian reservation or lands owned by any tribe, group, or band of Indians under Federal jurisdiction, except those specifically excepted from the provisions of sections 396a to 396g of this title, may, with the approval of the Secretary of the Interior, be leased for mining purposes, by authority of the tribal council or other authorized spokesmen for such Indians, for terms not to exceed ten years and as long thereafter as minerals are produced in paying quantities.May 11, 1938
notes: Repeal of Inconsistent Acts, , provided that:Act May 11, 1938, ch. 198, § 752 Stat. 348“All Act [Acts] or parts of Acts inconsistent herewith are hereby repealed.”
amendments: Repeal of Inconsistent Acts, , provided that:Act May 11, 1938, ch. 198, § 752 Stat. 348“All Act [Acts] or parts of Acts inconsistent herewith are hereby repealed.”
